channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Amendment Shifts NAB Case Appeals to Federal Constitutional Court, Affecting Imran Khan's Legal Future
item.description: A significant legal amendment has transferred the final appellate jurisdiction in National Accountability Bureau (NAB) cases from the Supreme Court to the newly established Federal Constitutional Cour...

item.title: Former Lawyer Peet Viljoen Battles Legal Challenges Amid Fraud Allegations
item.description: Former lawyer and reality TV star Peet Viljoen is facing a formidable legal challenge as he seeks bail amid allegations of fraud involving a R27.6 million property scheme. The prosecution has submitte...

item.title: Top Stock Picks for Investors: Zenith Bank, Unilever, and BUA Cement
item.description: The article highlights key stock recommendations for investors, focusing on Zenith Bank, Unilever, and BUA Cement. It explains that the Nigerian stock market has been performing well due to positive i...

item.title: BC Wildfires 2026: Live Map Tracks Active Fires and Evacuations
item.description: B.C.'s wildfire season for 2026 has officially begun, following a cooler start to the season than previous years. A live and interactive map is available to track active wildfires, evacuation orders, ...

item.title: Minister Chikunga initiates forensic review of NYDA’s financial mismanagement and governance issues
item.description: Minister Sindisiwe Chikunga has launched a forensic investigation into the National Youth Development Agency (NYDA) following allegations of severe governance failures, financial misconduct, and legal...

item.title: Major Storm Causes Power Outages in North Central Saskatchewan
item.description: A major storm struck north central Saskatchewan on July 11, 2026, causing widespread power outages that left the city of Prince Albert in darkness around 10:50 p.m. The storm brought destructive winds...

item.title: IMF approves 1.17 trillion shillings for Tanzania's economic reforms
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has approved the release of 443.9 million US dollars to Tanzania following successful completion of reviews under the country’s economic reform programme. This ma...

item.title: California legislators push to exempt film tax credit program from corporate tax cap
item.description: Over 30 California legislators are urging Governor Gavin Newsom to exempt the state's film and TV production incentive program from a newly imposed cap on corporate tax credits. The letter, addressed ...

item.title: Iran and US escalate conflict over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has claimed the Strait of Hormuz is closed following a night of intense US bombing targeting military facilities on Qeshm Island. The strikes come after Iran launched attacks on Gulf allies of th...

item.title: Adivasi identity and resistance: A historical analysis of cultural preservation in India
item.description: The article discusses a scholarly work that explores the complex socio-political dynamics of Adivasi communities in India. It highlights how these indigenous groups have historically resisted colonial...

item.title: IMF Disburses US$443.9 Million to Tanzania for Economic Reforms
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has approved the disbursement of US$443.9 million to Tanzania following the completion of reviews under the Extended Credit Facility (ECF) and Resilience and Sust...

item.title: Survivor of the Gardelegen Massacre Shares His Escape Story with U.S. Forces
item.description: The article recounts the harrowing events of the Gardelegen massacre in April 1945 during World War II. On April 13, SS troops and local auxiliaries herded over 1,000 slave laborers and concentration ...

item.title: LeBron James' Children: Bronny, Bryce, and Zhuri's Lives and Achievements
item.description: LeBron James, the NBA superstar, has three children with his wife Savannah: Bronny James Jr., Bryce Maximus James, and Zhuri Nova James. Bronny, born in 2004, joined the Los Angeles Lakers in 2024, be...

item.title: Federal Government’s Exam Fee Hike Sparks Concern Over School Dropouts
item.description: The Nigerian government's decision to increase examination fees for WAEC and NECO by 82% has sparked widespread concern among educators, parents, and students. The new fee of N50,000 per candidate has...

item.description: The article is an e-edition of BusinessDay newspaper dated July 13, 2026. It includes sections like Home, Tax Calculator, Companies, Markets, Economy, Real Estate, Agriculture, Aviation, Banking, Tran...

item.title: Operation Shaban intensifies: Over 114 militants killed in Balochistan operations
item.description: Security forces in Pakistan have intensified operations under 'Operation Shaban' in Balochistan, eliminating over 114 militants since July 5. Recent strikes resulted in the deaths of five more terrori...

item.title: Mad Cool Festival 2026 Concludes with Stellar Performances by Pulp, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, and David Byrne
item.description: The 10th-anniversary edition of the Mad Cool Festival in Madrid concluded with a spectacular final day of performances on July 11, 2026. The day featured a diverse lineup including rising stars and es...

item.title: Diesel and kerosene prices rise due to Middle East tensions
item.description: The Philippine government announced a significant increase in diesel and kerosene prices starting July 14, 2026, driven by escalating tensions in the Middle East. Energy Secretary Lance Spencer Yu rev...
